rationale: Both correctly validate the core codes.audio fix and its test coverage, matching the reviewer's approval rationale. X's top finding (containment fallback silently applies to all callers, not just opt-in tests) captures the same substantive concern the human reviewer raised about the fallback's scope, just framed behaviorally rather than as a docs nit, and its short_text-threshold observation is precise and grounded. Y surfaces more findings (unpopped legacy key, uninitialized test attribute, duplicate magic-number claim, IndexError nit, undocumented constants), but several are low-value nitpicks and one (duplicate `_SHORT_TEXT_THRESHOLD` at ~line 499) references code outside the diff that can't be verified here, diluting precision. Both are concretely actionable with file/line anchors.
